Variables: Rainfall, soil moisture (multiple depths), soil temperature, pressure, wind, solar radiation
Technical_Notes: CR1000/CR1000X loggers at 5-min cadence; aggregates NWS and regional stations; statewide TX network

Variables: Precipitation rate, precipitation source, quality index
Access_Method: NASA GES DISC / GEE / AWS Open Data
Technical_Notes: GPM Core Observatory (Ku/Ka-band DPR + GMI radiometer) + ~10 passive MW sensors + geo-IR Kalman filter morphing. V07B. Global coverage. Free EarthData login required.

Organization: PRISM Climate Group / Oregon State University
Variables: Precipitation, Tmin, Tmax, Tmean, dewpoint, VPD
Technical_Notes: Parameter-elevation Regression on Independent Slopes Model (PRISM). Weighted regression using station data + DEM. Explicitly models orographic enhancement and rain shadows — particularly suitable for Davis Mountains terrain. USDA official climatological data.

Variables: Volumetric soil moisture (m3/m3) top 5 cm, uncertainty, retrieval quality flag
Technical_Notes: SMAP L-band radiometer (1.4 GHz). NRT via LANCE within ~6h. Enhanced L2 product at 9 km. Arid/sparse-veg conditions in W. TX may require regional validation.

Variables: Total precipitation, soil water (4 layers), evaporation, SWE, snow depth, skin temperature
Access_Method: CDS API (cdsapi) / GEE / NSF NCAR GDEX
Technical_Notes: ERA5: 0.25 deg global hourly. ERA5-Land: 0.1 deg enhanced land surface. Soil water at 4 depths: 0-7cm / 7-28cm / 28-100cm / 100-289cm. 5-day latency exceeds 2-day target but unmatched for reanalysis depth.

Variables: Reflectivity, radial velocity, spectrum width, dual-pol variables
Access_Method: NCEI S3 (AWS) / NOAA Big Data Program
Technical_Notes: 160-station NEXRAD network. Nearest: KDFX Del Rio (~175 km SE), KEWX New Braunfels (~310 km E). Significant beam blockage expected from Davis Mountains. QPE quality degraded at this location.
